---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-15"
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


  Take a small sample for testing 
    
  - Compare different curve-fitting functions.
  - Explore alternative definitions of % increase in TCC.

```{r - for aoi}

# ---- inputs ----
#' 
#' the ids are taken from "D:\natcap\invest-mental-health\data\aoi\aoi_csv"
#' 
ids <- c(
  455000, 644000, 667000, 1245000, 1714000, 2507000, 2622000, 2743000,
  3651000, 4260000, 4261000, 4865000, 5363000, 1245975, 4824000, 4983470
)


ids <- sprintf("%07d", as.integer(ids)) # make sure the GEOID_PLACE are 7 digits



src_dir  <- "G:/Shared drives/invest-health/City500/aoi_each_with_tract"
dst_dir  <- "G:/Shared drives/invest-health/City16/aoi_each_with_tract"
recursive <- FALSE # set TRUE if you want to search subfolders

# ---- prep ----
if (!dir.exists(dst_dir)) dir.create(dst_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# List all files (any type)
files <- list.files(src_dir, full.names = TRUE, recursive = recursive, all.files = FALSE)
fnames <- basename(files)

# Build safe regex for each ID: match as a whole number token in the name (ignores extension)
# e.g., "..._42.", ".42_", "-42-", "42." etc., but NOT "...142..." or "420..."
id_patterns <- paste0("(?<![0-9])", ids, "(?![0-9])")

# Which files match any ID? (case-insensitive)
matched_idx <- Reduce(`|`, lapply(id_patterns, function(p) grepl(p, fnames, perl = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)))
matches <- files[matched_idx]

# Optional: de-duplicate (if recursive=TRUE different paths may repeat basenames)
matches <- unique(matches)

# ---- copy ----
ok <- file.copy(matches, dst_dir, overwrite = FALSE)

# ---- simple report ----
data.frame(
  file = basename(matches),
  copied = ok,
  from = dirname(matches),
  to = normalizePath(dst_dir, winslash = "/")
)

```




```{r - for ndvi}

src_dir  <- "G:/Shared drives/invest-health/City500/ndvi_us500"
dst_dir  <- "G:/Shared drives/invest-health/City16/ndvi_us500"
recursive <- FALSE # set TRUE if you want to search subfolders

# ---- prep ----
if (!dir.exists(dst_dir)) dir.create(dst_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# List all files (any type)
files <- list.files(src_dir, full.names = TRUE, recursive = recursive, all.files = FALSE)
fnames <- basename(files)

# Build safe regex for each ID: match as a whole number token in the name (ignores extension)
# e.g., "..._42.", ".42_", "-42-", "42." etc., but NOT "...142..." or "420..."
id_patterns <- paste0("(?<![0-9])", ids, "(?![0-9])")

# Which files match any ID? (case-insensitive)
matched_idx <- Reduce(`|`, lapply(id_patterns, function(p) grepl(p, fnames, perl = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)))
matches <- files[matched_idx]

# Optional: de-duplicate (if recursive=TRUE different paths may repeat basenames)
matches <- unique(matches)

# ---- copy ----
ok <- file.copy(matches, dst_dir, overwrite = FALSE)

# ---- simple report ----
data.frame(
  file = basename(matches),
  copied = ok,
  from = dirname(matches),
  to = normalizePath(dst_dir, winslash = "/")
)

```
